subject: refactor(cleanup): drop dead exports; extract StyleTab cell-override helpers
body:
clean-trigger findings resolved:

- downloadDiagnosticImage: Inlined into its sole DEV-gated call site in
  ExportButtons.jsx and removed from exportHelpers.js. The 3-line helper
  shipped in every prod bundle despite being DEV-only. Inlining drops
  the `saveAs` import from exportHelpers.js entirely (ExportButtons
  already imports it), so no dev-only code reaches production.

- MIME_TYPES: Dropped the `export` keyword — the constant was only
  referenced internally by captureAsBlob and imported-but-unused in
  ExportButtons. Removed the unused import and the direct unit test
  (an internal constant doesn't need a public contract test; format
  behavior is covered by manual export tests in TESTING_GUIDE.md).

- StyleTab cell-override duplication: Added `updateCellBackground` and
  `updateCellFrame` helpers mirroring the existing `updateCellOverlay` /
  `updateCellPadding` pattern. Replaces 5 inline `{ ...obj, [key]: ... }`
  blocks with one-line callbacks. Kept two helpers instead of one
  generic because backgrounds (scalar value) and frames (merged object)
  have different semantics, and each writes to a different parent
  callback (onLayoutChange vs onFrameChange).

- pruneOrphanedKeys: Reviewed and kept as-is. The prior finding claimed
  a single call site, but it has 5 inside cleanupOrphanedCells. Inlining
  would duplicate the NaN-guarded loop 5x. Already module-private.

Net -18 LOC. All 138 unit tests pass, ESLint clean, prod build succeeds.

Tags: cleanup, refactor, exports, style-tab, export-helpers
Complexity: 2
Urgency: 2
Impact: internal
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: clean-sweep-2026-04
Semver: patch
